title: Code of Conduct
text: grainsizeR follows simple contribution standard: respectful, specific, evidence-focused. Keep discussions code, documentation, data provenance, scientific methods constructive. Harassment, personal attacks, knowingly disruptive behavior acceptable issues, pull requests, project discussions. Project maintainers may edit, close, remove contributions outside project scope inconsistent standard.

title: Basic grainsizeR Workflow
text: Long-format input one row per sample grain-size class.
code: long_file <- system.file("extdata", "grain.long.csv", package = "grainsizeR")  gs <- read_gsd(   long_file,   format = "long",   sample_col = "sample",   size_col = "size",   value_col = "proportion",   size_unit = "mm",   value_type = "proportion" )  head(gs) #> # A tibble: 6 × 13 #>   sample_id bin_id raw_size_um size_lower_um size_upper_um size_mid_um #>   <chr>      <int>       <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 S01            1      2000          2000              NA        NA   #> 2 S01            2      1000          1000            2000      1414.  #> 3 S01            3       500           500            1000       707.  #> 4 S01            4       250           250             500       354.  #> 5 S01            5       125           125             250       177.  #> 6 S01            6        62.5          62.5           125        88.4 #> # ℹ 7 more variables: size_mid_phi <dbl>, retained_percent <dbl>, #> #   cum_finer_percent <dbl>, cum_coarser_percent <dbl>, is_open_lower <lgl>, #> #   is_open_upper <lgl>, measurement_method <chr>

title: Basic grainsizeR Workflow
text: Wide input stores size classes rows samples columns. Terminal fine rows <0.0625 supported become open-ended fine classes. G2Sd-style wide tables often store particle sizes row names samples columns. Convert row names size column use size_unit = "auto" size_unit = "um" importing micrometre-scale labels.

title: Grain-Size Analysis Workflow
text: plot_distribution() returns ggplot object supports metric phi axis scales. function can overlay cumulative percent finer retained size-class bars GRADISTAT-style combined display. examples use dry-sieve wide dataset plotted samples align README GRADISTAT-style showcase. Metric displays use particle size millimetres log-scaled x-axis default, major breaks 0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1, 10 mm. Distribution bars centered particle-size classes. Use particle_unit = "um" micrometre axes. Distribution cumulative plots show one sample time; loop samples arrange returned plots externally multi-sample figures. Lower open-ended classes displayed 0.0015 mm, 1.5 um, plotting ; calculations unchanged.
code: plot_distribution(gs_wide, sample = "S01") plot_distribution(gs_wide, sample = "S01", cumulative = TRUE) plot_distribution(gs_wide, x_scale = "phi", type = "line", sample = "S01")

title: Method Validation and Numerical Assumptions
text: package includes two example files: grain.long.csv sieve + hydrometer example stored tidy long table finer particle-size resolution hydrometer data added. grain.wide.csv dry-sieve example stored multi-sample wide table useful coarse gravel/sand/mud summaries.
code: long_file <- system.file("extdata", "grain.long.csv", package = "grainsizeR") wide_file <- system.file("extdata", "grain.wide.csv", package = "grainsizeR")  gs_long <- read_gsd(   long_file,   format = "long",   sample_col = "sample",   size_col = "size",   value_col = "proportion",   size_unit = "mm",   value_type = "proportion" )  gs_wide <- read_gsd(   wide_file,   format = "wide",   size_col = 1,   size_unit = "mm",   value_type = "percent" )

title: Method Validation and Numerical Assumptions
text: Input rows describe retained material grain-size classes. numeric label used class boundary. Terminal coarse fine classes can open-ended. Open-ended classes retained data structure, missing boundary silently replaced zero infinity interpolation.

title: Method Validation and Numerical Assumptions
text: long wide examples contain sample IDs. , file names describe table layout, measurement method. Coarse Wentworth-style gravel, sand, mud summaries agree two files represent aggregate behavior 62.5 um. Clay/silt-level schemes USDA, ISSS, UK SSEW require thresholds 2, 20, 50, 60, 63 um; better resolved finite measured boundaries bracket thresholds. Instrument outputs laser cumulative percent-finer curves may require preprocessing import. read_gsd() expects retained proportions retained percentages size class, cumulative percent passing.

what: What GRADISTAT and G2Sd Workflows Usually Provide
title: Replacing GRADISTAT and G2Sd Workflows
text: Typical workflows include importing retained grain-size data, calculating D-values graphical statistics, describing sediment parameters, checking quality cautions, classifying texture, composing sediment names, producing distribution, cumulative, fraction, texture ternary plots.

title: Replacing GRADISTAT and G2Sd Workflows
text: Short aliases gs_fw57(), gs_frac(), gs_diag(), gs_qc() available interactive work. full names table remain clearest choices reproducible scripts method descriptions.

text: wide dry-sieve example used GRADISTAT-style gravel-sand-mud workflow. long example includes finer fractions used USDA texture examples. Open-ended tails silently extrapolated; calls need extrapolation use extrapolate = "warn_linear" explicitly.

title: Replacing GRADISTAT and G2Sd Workflows
text: USDA major texture classification uses sand, silt, clay percentages. USDA sand-size modifier subclasses remain future work. GRADISTAT texture classification supports gravel-sand-mud sand-silt-clay -gravel bases.
code: usda_fractions <- suppressWarnings(gs_fractions_wide(   gs,   scheme = "usda_tt",   normalize = "fine_earth",   extrapolate = "warn_linear" ))  usda_samples <- data.frame(   sample_id = usda_fractions$sample_id,   sand = usda_fractions$sand_percent,   silt = usda_fractions$silt_percent,   clay = usda_fractions$clay_percent ) usda_components <- c("sand", "silt", "clay") usda_samples <- usda_samples[   stats::complete.cases(usda_samples[usda_components]) &     rowSums(usda_samples[usda_components] >= 0 & usda_samples[usda_components] <= 100) == 3 &     abs(rowSums(usda_samples[usda_components]) - 100) < 1e-6, ]  head(classify_texture(usda_samples, scheme = "usda_tt", method = "rules")) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   sample_id  sand  silt  clay texture_class_id texture_class #>   <chr>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>            <chr>         #> 1 S01        87.5 12.5      0 sand             sand          #> 2 S02       100    0        0 sand             sand          #> 3 S03       100    0        0 sand             sand          #> 4 S04        90.9  9.13     0 sand             sand          #> 5 S05        92.2  7.76     0 sand             sand          #> 6 S06       100    0        0 sand             sand          #> # ℹ 5 more variables: classification_method <chr>, rule_status <chr>, #> #   all_rule_matches <chr>, rule_conflict <lgl>, rule_gap <lgl> gradistat_fractions <- suppressWarnings(gs_fractions_wide(gs_wide, scheme = "gravel_sand_mud"))  gsm <- data.frame(   sample_id = gradistat_fractions$sample_id,   gravel = gradistat_fractions$gravel_percent,   sand = gradistat_fractions$sand_percent,   mud = gradistat_fractions$mud_percent ) gsm <- gsm[stats::complete.cases(gsm[c("gravel", "sand", "mud")]), ]  ssc <- data.frame(   sample_id = c("A", "B", "C"),   sand = c(95, 60, 20),   silt = c(3, 30, 60),   clay = c(2, 10, 20) )  gsm_classified <- classify_texture(   head(gsm, 6),   scheme = "gradistat",   method = "rules",   basis = "gravel_sand_mud",   include_sediment_name = TRUE )  ssc_classified <- classify_texture(   ssc,   scheme = "gradistat",   method = "rules",   basis = "sand_silt_clay_no_gravel" )  gsm_classified #> # what: Why Some Fine-End D-Values Remain Unresolved
title: Table layouts and measurement workflows
text: Open-ended terminal classes provide finite lower upper boundaries. D5 falls inside open fine tail, D95 falls inside open coarse tail, default behavior report value unresolved. Users may explicitly request extrapolation, extrapolation decision documented.

title: Texture Classification
text: classify_texture() provides two texture-classification paths: USDA 12-class major texture classification sand, silt, clay percentages. GRADISTAT-style rule classification gravel-sand-mud textural groups -gravel sand-silt-clay mini texture classes. Generic classification user-supplied ternary texture polygons. USDA path rule-based. require bundled USDA polygon data, USDA polygon dataset included package data. GRADISTAT path also rule-based. uses source-grounded decision tables user-provided GRADISTAT v8 workbook textural-output context described Blott Pye (2001). Sediment-name composition available separate step optional output. GRADISTAT ternary plotting available plot_texture_ternary().
code: library(grainsizeR) long_file <- system.file("extdata", "grain.long.csv", package = "grainsizeR") wide_file <- system.file("extdata", "grain.wide.csv", package = "grainsizeR")  long_gs <- read_gsd(long_file) wide_gs <- read_gsd(wide_file, format = "wide")

title: Texture Classification
text: Use scheme = "usda_tt" method = "rules" validated USDA major-class workflow. USDA texture ternary plotting available plot_texture_ternary(). stable plot_texture_triangle() function name remains available compatibility. bundled long-format example includes finer fractions can used USDA texture workflow. open-ended tails require explicit extrapolation resolved sand, silt, clay percentages; example makes assumption visible. USDA ternary plots draw 12 major-class boundaries package’s independently expressed rule logic annotate ternary percentage axes directly, without ordinary Cartesian x/y tick labels. USDA ternary plots place percent sand, percent silt, percent clay outside corresponding triangle sides.  Input percentages must numeric, finite, 0 100, sum approximately 100. classify_texture() silently normalize invalid sand, silt, clay sums. Data frames ternary left, right, top columns also accepted USDA rules. interpreted left = sand, right = silt, top = clay.
code: samples <- data.frame(   sample_id = c("A", "B", "C"),   sand = c(85, 40, 20),   silt = c(10, 40, 20),   clay = c(5, 20, 60) )  classify_texture(samples, scheme = "usda_tt", method = "rules") #> # A tibble: 3 × 11 #>   sample_id  sand  silt  clay texture_class_id texture_class #>   <chr>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>            <chr>         #> 1 A            85    10     5 loamy_sand       loamy sand    #> 2 B            40    40    20 loam             loam          #> 3 C            20    20    60 clay             clay          #> # ℹ 5 more variables: classification_method <chr>, rule_status <chr>, #> #   all_rule_matches <chr>, rule_conflict <lgl>, rule_gap <lgl> usda_fractions <- suppressWarnings(gs_fractions_wide(   long_gs,   scheme = "usda_tt",   normalize = "fine_earth",   extrapolate = "warn_linear" ))  usda_example <- data.frame(   sample_id = usda_fractions$sample_id,   sand = usda_fractions$sand_percent,   silt = usda_fractions$silt_percent,   clay = usda_fractions$clay_percent ) usda_components <- c("sand", "silt", "clay") usda_example <- usda_example[   stats::complete.cases(usda_example[usda_components]) &     rowSums(usda_example[usda_components] >= 0 & usda_example[usda_components] <= 100) == 3 &     abs(rowSums(usda_example[usda_components]) - 100) < 1e-6, ]  head(classify_texture(usda_example, scheme = "usda_tt", method = "rules")) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   sample_id  sand  silt  clay texture_class_id texture_class #>   <chr>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>            <chr>         #> 1 S01        87.5 12.5      0 sand             sand          #> 2 S02       100    0        0 sand             sand          #> 3 S03       100    0        0 sand             sand          #> 4 S04        90.9  9.13     0 sand             sand          #> 5 S05        92.2  7.76     0 sand             sand          #> 6 S06       100    0        0 sand             sand          #> # ℹ 5 more variables: classification_method <chr>, rule_status <chr>, #> #   all_rule_matches <chr>, rule_conflict <lgl>, rule_gap <lgl>  plot_texture_ternary(usda_example, scheme = "usda_tt", labels = FALSE)

title: Texture Classification
text: Use scheme = "gradistat" method = "rules" GRADISTAT-style texture classification. gravel_sand_mud basis requires gravel, sand, mud percentage columns. bundled dry-sieve wide example supports GRADISTAT-style gravel-sand-mud workflow. sand_silt_clay_no_gravel basis requires sand, silt, clay percentage columns. GRADISTAT rule classification returns texture_class_id, texture_class, classification_method, classification_status, ternary_basis, notes, ratio audit column. Input percentages must sum approximately 100 selected basis; grainsizeR silently normalize invalid sums. TEXTURAL GROUP SEDIMENT NAME distinct GRADISTAT-style outputs. Sediment-name composition can requested GRADISTAT classification include_sediment_name = TRUE, added afterward gs_gradistat_sediment_name(). dominant size-subclass columns missing, textural group returned partial sediment name. supported bases can drawn texture ternary plots. dry-sieve wide example, sample labels suppressed keep GRADISTAT gravel-sand-mud class labels readable. gravel-sand-mud basis, ternary plot places Gravel top, Mud lower-left apex, Sand lower-right apex. Gravel percentage guides sand/mud ratio guides drawn ternary annotations rather Cartesian axes; unlike USDA display, GRADISTAT apex labels remain vertices.   Distribution, cumulative, fraction, USDA texture ternary, GRADISTAT texture ternary plots R-native functional replacements, Excel visual clones. plot_texture_ternary() preferred plotting name new examples. plot_texture_triangle() remains available compatibility existing code.

ℹ 14 more variables: classification_method <chr>, #> #   classification_status <chr>, notes <chr>, sand_mud_ratio <dbl>, #> #   textural_group_class_id <chr>, textural_group <chr>, #> #   mini_texture_class_id <chr>, mini_texture_class <chr>, #> # title: Sediment Grain-Size Analysis Tools
text: grainsizeR provides R tools sediment grain-size analysis. reads retained grain-size distributions, validates gsd_tbl objects, calculates D-values common summary statistics, builds particle-size fraction summaries, classifies supported texture systems, creates distribution, cumulative, fraction, texture ternary plots.

title: Sediment Grain-Size Analysis Tools
text: grainsizeR active development. Built-official texture polygon datasets bundled yet; USDA major texture GRADISTAT-style texture classification available internal rule helpers.
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("Gavin987/grainsizeR")

title: Sediment Grain-Size Analysis Tools
text: bundled examples include wide dry-sieve table long-format sieve-plus-hydrometer table. imported, use object summary plotting functions:
code: library(grainsizeR)  wide_path <- system.file("extdata", "grain.wide.csv", package = "grainsizeR") long_path <- system.file("extdata", "grain.long.csv", package = "grainsizeR")  wide <- read_gsd(wide_path, format = "wide") long <- read_gsd(long_path) gs_diagnostics(wide, output = "summary") gs_d_values(long, probs = c(10, 50, 90), extrapolate = "warn_linear") gs_folk_ward(long, extrapolate = "warn_linear") gradistat_components <- gs_fractions(wide, scheme = "gravel_sand_mud") gs_fractions_wide(wide, scheme = "gravel_sand_mud")

title: Sediment Grain-Size Analysis Tools

text: x valid gsd_tbl object, USDA rule classification data frame numeric sand, silt, clay percentage columns. Data frames ternary left, right, top columns also accepted USDA rules mapped left = sand, right = silt, top = clay. polygon classification, x must gsd_tbl. polygons User-supplied texture polygon data. legacy positional argument equivalent texture_polygons. scheme Texture classification scheme. Use "usda_tt" method = "rules" method = "auto" USDA major texture rules. Use "gradistat" method = "rules" method = "auto" GRADISTAT-style rule classification. non-USDA schemes require user-supplied polygons built-texture polygon datasets bundled. method Classification method. "auto" uses USDA rules scheme = "usda_tt" GRADISTAT rules scheme = "gradistat" polygons supplied, polygon classification polygons supplied. "rules" selects supported rule classifier. "polygon" selects user-supplied polygon classification. texture_polygons User-supplied texture polygon data. normalize Normalization mode passed gs_fractions_wide(). interpolation_scale Interpolation scale passed gs_fractions_wide(). unresolved Unresolved-threshold behavior passed gs_fractions_wide(). extrapolate Extrapolation behavior passed gs_fractions_wide(). components Optional named character vector mapping left, right, top ternary axes fraction components. basis Rule-classification basis. scheme = "gradistat", use "gravel_sand_mud" gravel, sand, mud columns, "sand_silt_clay_no_gravel" sand, silt, clay columns. USDA classification ignores argument. include_sediment_name Logical. GRADISTAT rule classification, TRUE appends GRADISTAT-style sediment-name fields using gs_gradistat_sediment_name(). Missing subclass columns produce partial sediment-name status instead invented modifiers. USDA polygon classification ignore argument.

what: grainsizeR: sediment grain-size analysis tools — grainsizeR-package
title: grainsizeR: sediment grain-size analysis tools — grainsizeR-package
text: grainsizeR provides tools sediment grain-size analysis R. reads retained grain-size distributions, stores validated gsd_tbl objects, calculates boundary-based grain-size statistics, creates common plots distribution, cumulative, fraction, ternary texture workflows.

title: grainsizeR: sediment grain-size analysis tools — grainsizeR-package
text: Input data can supplied long format, one row per sample size class, wide format, size classes rows samples columns. Values may retained proportions, retained percentages, weights normalized within sample. grainsizeR, D_p grain size p percent sample finer. example, D50 median grain size percent-finer curve. typical workflow : Read data read_gsd() read_gsd_wide(). Inspect validate resulting gsd_tbl. Calculate D-values gs_d_values(). Calculate additional grain-size indices gs_grain_size_indices(). Calculate Folk Ward statistics gs_folk_ward(). Optionally calculate moments explicit open-end handling using gs_moments(). Calculate fractions gs_fractions() gs_fractions_wide(). Plot results plot_distribution(), plot_cumulative(), plot_fractions(), plot_texture_ternary(). Optionally classify samples user-supplied texture polygons. Open-ended terminal classes silently treated bounded intervals. Calculations require values inside open tails require explicit extrapolation unresolved-value handling. Built-official texture polygon datasets bundled yet. package implement civil engineering classification systems.

title: Calculate grain-size fraction percentages — gs_fractions
text: Scheme thresholds need match observed grain-size boundaries. thresholds 0.002, 0.020, 0.050, 0.060, 0.063 mm bracketed finite class boundaries, percent-finer values interpolated cumulative curve. Fraction texture functions automatically use normalized particle-size scale gsd_tbl; users need specify millimetres micrometres import. Thresholds largest observed finite boundary resolve 100 percent finer, thresholds smallest observed finite boundary resolve 0 percent finer. returns absent particle-size classes zero rather NA, complete schemes close 100 percent samples whose retained percentages sum 100. NA reserved thresholds genuinely unresolved inside finite observed size range. Fraction schemes extrapolate unless extrapolate = "warn_linear" passed explicitly.

Preferred values "mm" millimetres "um" micrometres. Aliases "milli" "micro" also accepted. sample Optional sample selector. character value selects sample ID; numeric value selects one-based sample index using order samples appear x. sample_id Optional character vector sample identifiers include. Kept backward compatibility; use sample new code. show_percentiles Optional logical numeric vector D-value percentiles mark plot. TRUE marks D10, D50, D90. extrapolate Extrapolation behavior passed gs_d_values() show_percentiles supplied. default "error", plot_cumulative() retries marker placement "warn_linear" requested percentile falls just outside finite boundary curve; affects plotted marker layer. percentile_color Color percentile marker crosses. percentile_size Size percentile marker crosses. percentile_stroke Stroke width percentile marker crosses. facet_by_sample Deprecated. Cumulative plots single-sample displays; use sample_id select one sample, loop samples, arrange returned plots externally another plotting package.

title: grainsizeR 0.0.0.9000
text: Added long wide retained grain-size import, validated gsd_tbl objects, diagnostics, D-values, percent-finer interpolation, grain-size indices, Folk Ward statistics, moment statistics, modes, D-spread descriptors, parameter descriptions, quality flags, summary-table workflows. Added particle-size fraction schemes, wide fraction summaries, plotting helpers retained distributions, cumulative curves, fractions, GRADISTAT- inspired summaries, texture ternary plots. Added GRADISTAT-style texture classification, sediment-name composition, gravel-sand-mud sand-silt-clay -gravel ternary plots. Added USDA 12-class major texture classification classify_texture(..., scheme = "usda_tt", method = "rules") USDA ternary plotting without depending external texture plotting packages. Added user-supplied texture polygon validation classification workflows; built-official texture polygon datasets bundled yet. Added workflow vignettes grain-size analysis, texture classification, replacing GRADISTAT/G2Sd-style workflows, table layouts measurement workflows, method validation, user-supplied texture polygons. Added convenience aliases common workflows, including plot_texture_ternary() preferred texture ternary plotting name retaining compatibility aliases. Improved log-scaled distribution cumulative plots single-sample behavior, particle_unit controls, centered distribution bars, plain tick labels, plotting-lower open-tail display positions. Improved plotting ergonomics numeric sample selection, clearer cumulative percentile markers, texture ternary point controls labels, constant aesthetics, grouped color mapping. Updated bundled long wide example CSV data regenerated README examples figures bundled files. Refined GRADISTAT USDA ternary plot guides, labels, boundaries, README showcase figures; README PNG outputs standardized 1000 px wide. Prepared GitHub pre-release documentation repository templates, including contribution guidance, issue templates, citation metadata, security notes, check workflows. Added MIT licensing public provenance policy future official texture polygon datasets.
